mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations and tighten financial conditions, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS exceeds 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positively correlated with rate levels.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5%, labor market slack signals weakening consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or UNRATE threshold shows no predictive power in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint RSAFS and HOUST growth fails to predict positive 60d returns, with interaction term coefficient statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in walk-forward regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during post-shock recovery phases reflects expanding capacity utilization and operational leverage, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO momentum shows negative or zero predictive power for 5-day returns specifically during post-shock recovery periods, or regime classification yields no alpha separation.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPIAUCSL index implies year-over-year inflation exceeds 3.0%, markets price in aggressive central bank tightening and margin compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 55% of high-inflation triggered months, or CPIAUCSL threshold loses predictive significance after controlling for rate expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payrolls growth combined with a declining unemployment rate signals a tightening labor market that boosts wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction between PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline fails to generate positive 20d returns, with combined signal showing negative Sharpe ratio in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth reflects robust economic expansion and corporate profit momentum, which consistently supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rate shows negative or zero correlation with 60-day forward returns over any 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, rising industrial production alongside higher federal funds rates indicates supply-side resilience that outpaces financing costs, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative in > 60% of instances where both INDPRO rises and FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, or interaction effect is statistically insignificant in regime-filtered samples.
